note: APPEND-ONLY store. The weekly job only ADDS items (autoDetected=true, use=''). It never edits or removes existing entries. Human-curated 'use' lines are added by editing this file directly and are never touched by automation.

issues.s: Temporary discontinuation while a new manufacturer awaits certification; delisted Jun/Jul 2026, resolution expected 01/12/2026.
issues.use: Trusts using BioFlo PICCs need an alternative until at least December 2026 — a live opening for other PICC suppliers.

issues.s: Discontinued through rationalisation; progressive delisting since Jan 2025, all codes delisted by 31/07/2026.
issues.use: Any trust still on Venflon must convert now — an immediate switching conversation for every cannula supplier.

issues.s: Global product removal for material-fatigue leak risk (FSN updated 15/05/2025); unused stock destroyed.
issues.use: Context for PICC conversations — ask what the trust moved to and whether the replacement is bedding in well.

issues.p: Cook FSN covering several sets, including the Spectrum Central Venous Catheter Set
issues.s: Field safety notice dated 05/03/2026 (MHRA ref 38698628) listing multiple Cook products including the Spectrum Central Venous Catheter Set; the MHRA listing does not state the nature of the issue — read the FSN itself before quoting it.
issues.use: A live Cook FSN touches the Spectrum central venous catheter set — ask your central line accounts whether they were affected and what action they took, without assuming the fault.

issues.p: Disposable Infusion Set (A211M, A211M1F, A211M5F1, A311M, A311M021F, A212)
issues.s: Field safety notice dated 01/07/2026 (MHRA ref 40179552), listed on the MHRA's 13–17 July 2026 FSN page; the listing does not state the nature of the issue — read the FSN itself before quoting it. The same page also lists a Medtronic Sphere-360 catheter FSN (ref 40194215).
issues.use: An open field safety notice on a disposable infusion set is a reason to ask your infusion accounts whether they hold Medcaptain stock and what they would move to, without assuming the fault.

issues.p: Interventional urology portfolio (110 NHS Supply Chain lines)
issues.s: Field Safety Notice 16/12/2024: withdrawal over possible sterility issue; 42 lines suspended, 3 permanently deactivated.
issues.use: Ask which lines your accounts lost and whether replacements have fully restored service.

issues.s: Ongoing supply issue; seven codes still suspended as of May 2026; 12 PTFE-coated Foley lines discontinued and delisted.
issues.use: Affected trusts need alternative Foley supply now — a direct opening for other indwelling-catheter suppliers.

issues.s: Wellead range delisted from 01/05/2026; Folysil reinstated as the sole Coloplast silicone Foley option.
issues.use: Trusts that used Wellead have just changed product — worth asking how the transition has gone.

issues.s: SUSPECTED FALSE POSITIVE — no continence, urology or vascular access product on this page. Checked 20/07/2026; safe to delete.
SUSPECTED FALSE POSITIVE — likely matched on Siemens Atellica CH Urine Albumin, a laboratory IVD reagent, not a continence product. Checked 20/07/2026; safe to delete.

issues.p: Field Safety Notice Boston Scientific Medical Device Correction NephroMax™ High Pressure Nephrostomy Balloon Catheters (ICN 3416)
issues.s: Auto-detected from NHS Supply Chain ICN - open the source for details and verify before use.

issues.p: ChloraPrep 2% w/v / 70% v/v cutaneous solution, 1mL applicators
issues.s: Class 2 medicines recall issued 13/07/2026 (EL(26)A/34) for a potential breach of sterility in the packaging process; covers the listed lots with expiry dates up to and including 05/2029, with stock to be quarantined and returned to suppliers.
issues.use: Line-insertion accounts have had their 1mL ChloraPrep applicators pulled — ask what they are prepping with now and whether that stand-in is going to stick.

issues.s: Class 2 medicines recall issued 02/06/2026 (EL(26)A/26) for a potential breach of sterility in the packaging process, covering batches with expiry dates up to and including 02/2028 (ChloraPrep 1mL) and 01/2028 (ChloraPrep Frepp 1.5mL); a wider recall followed on 13/07/2026.
issues.use: The first of two ChloraPrep recalls this year — useful for showing an account how long their skin-prep supply has been disrupted, rather than treating July as a one-off.

issues.co: Medline Industries LP and Kimal PLC
issues.p: Namic angiographic control syringes with rotating adaptor, and Namic White Star off-handle manifolds, supplied within Kimal procedure packs
issues.s: MHRA device safety information DSI/2026/002, issued 19/05/2026 and updated 22/05/2026: the rotating adaptor on affected Namic syringes may unscrew during use (risk of disconnection, blood loss, infection and air embolism), and affected manifolds may carry foreign particulates in the fluid pathway; affected products are to be removed and destroyed unless use is unavoidable in an urgent procedure with no alternative.
issues.use: Cath lab and interventional radiology accounts have had recalled components pulled out of their Kimal packs — ask what pack they are running now and whether the substitution has settled.

issues.p: Skin disinfectant applicators DMK000 (1mL, 2% chlorhexidine gluconate) and MRB303 (1.5mL, 2% chlorhexidine gluconate)
issues.s: NHS Supply Chain safety alert (ICN 3355) issued 04/06/2026 and updated 14/07/2026 to extend the affected DMK000 lots to expiry 05/2029; affected stock is to be quarantined and returned no later than 24/07/2026, with MRB306 and MRB494 listed as alternatives subject to each organisation's own clinical suitability review.
issues.use: NHS Supply Chain has named MRB306 and MRB494 as the alternatives — worth finding out which one your accounts have moved to before the 24 July return deadline passes.

issues.co: Regent Medical Limited / Mölnlycke Health Care
issues.s: Class 2 medicines recall issued 23/03/2026 (EL(26)A/15) for microbial contamination (Burkholderia cepacia) found during routine microbiological monitoring at the manufacturing facility; batches 5156042, 5156043 and 5156093 (all expiry 28/11/2028) to be quarantined and returned.
issues.use: A pre-operative skin wash rather than a vascular access or continence product, so background only — but it sits alongside the ChloraPrep recalls as evidence that antiseptic supply has been unsettled this year.

issues.p: FSW288 — needlefree transfer spike take set with Swan-Lock needlefree valve
issues.s: NHS Supply Chain supply issue notice (ICN 3368) dated 16/06/2026, caused by a manufacturing problem at the supplier; FSB399 (needlefree bag spike with SmartSite valve) was offered as an alternative subject to local clinical review, and the notice records the product as available to order again from 15/07/2026.
issues.use: The gap has closed, but accounts that switched to FSB399 in June may not have switched back — worth asking which needlefree connector they are actually using now.

issues.p: Supply Issues Becton Dickinson UK Ltd Administration Set Alaris GP Volumetric Pump FKA321 (ICN 3369)
issues.s: Auto-detected from NHS Supply Chain ICN - names a tracked product. Open the source and verify before use.
